data.title: Okta's Q4 results surpass estimates, but guidance appears mixed
data.description:
Okta (OKTA) reported its fourth-quarter fiscal 2026 financial results post-market on Wednesday, which surpassed market expectations, but its first-quarter guidance fell short. 

However, its full fiscal year 2027 outlook surpassed consensus estimates. Shares were down about 1% during early after-hours trading.

For the quarter ended January 31, the identity management company reported adjusted earnings per share of $0.90 versus the consensus estimate of $0.85. GAAP EPS was $0.36 compared to the $0.23 estimate. 

Revenue for the fourth quarter increased 12% year over year to $761M, which was more than the $749.5M estimate. The company reported an adjusted gross margin of 82%, which was just ahead of the 81.6% estimate. 

Looking ahead, Okta expects first-quarter fiscal 2027 revenue to range from $749M to $753M, with a midpoint of $751M, which is less than the $754M estimate. It expects adjusted EPS to have a midpoint of $0.85, which is also less than the $0.87 estimate.

For the entirety of fiscal 2027, Okta projects revenue ranging from $3.17B to $3.19B with a midpoint that is just above the $3.17B estimate. It projects adjusted EPS to range from $3.74 to $3.82, with a midpoint of $3.78, which is stronger than the $3.66 estimate.

"AI is redefining the future of software and creating a critical need to secure AI agents, a challenge Okta was built to solve," said Okta CEO and co-founder Todd McKinnon. "As the only independent and neutral identity platform, we are uniquely positioned to secure every identity—from humans to AI agents—while providing our customers across the public and private sectors the flexibility to innovate with confidence in the early stages of this new era."

data.title: Rigetti Computing slides as Q4 results miss estimates
data.description:
Rigetti Computing (RGTI) shares fell 3.5% in extended trading on Wednesday after the quantum computing firm reported fourth-quarter results that missed Wall Street's estimates.

For the period ending Dec. 31, Rigetti said it lost an adjusted $0.03 per share as revenue came in at $1.87M. Analysts had expected the company to lose an adjusted $0.03 per share on $2.37M in revenue.

“Looking ahead, we remain focused on executing our roadmap, including the deployment of our 108-qubit system at 99.5% median two-qubit gate fidelity and advancing toward larger-scale systems as we work steadily toward quantum advantage," said Dr. Subodh Kulkarni, Rigetti's CEO, in a statement. "We anticipate significant first-quarter year-over-year revenue growth driven by a portion of the previously announced $5.7 million Novera on-premises system purchase orders expected to ship in Q1.” 

The company will host a conference call at 5 p.m. EST to discuss the results.

data.title: Red Violet Non-GAAP EPS of $0.21 beats by $0.07, revenue of $23.4M beats by $1.4M
data.description:
• Red Violet press release (RDVT): Q4 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.21 beats by $0.07.
  • Revenue of $23.4M (+19.6% Y/Y) beats by $1.4M.
  • Gross profit increased 23% to $16.8 million. Gross margin increased to 72% from 70%.
  • Adjusted gross profit increased 21% to $19.5 million. Adjusted gross margin increased to 83% from 82%.
  • Net income increased 226% to $2.8 million, which resulted in earnings of $0.20 and $0.19 per basic and diluted share, respectively. Net income margin increased to 12% from 4%.
  • Adjusted EBITDA increased 33% to $5.9 million. Adjusted EBITDA margin increased to 25% from 23%.

data.title: Grocery Outlet Non-GAAP EPS of $0.19 misses by $0.02, revenue of $1.22B misses by $10M
data.description:
• Grocery Outlet press release (GO): Q4 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.19 misses by $0.02.
  • Revenue of $1.22B (+10.9% Y/Y) misses by $10M.
  • Net sales increased by 7.3% to $4.69 billion.
  • Comparable store sales increased by 0.5% on a 52-week basis.
  • Gross margin was 30.3% compared to 30.2% last year.
  • Operating loss was $221.7 million, which included $113.8 million in non-cash impairment of long-lived assets, $45.9 million in restructuring charges and $149.0 million in non-cash goodwill impairment.
  • Net loss was $224.9 million, or $(2.30) per diluted share, compared to net income of $39.5 million, or $0.40 per diluted share last year. Adjusted net income(1) was $75.2 million, or $0.76 diluted adjusted earnings per share(1), compared to $76.3 million, or $0.77 diluted adjusted earnings per share(1) last year.
  • Adjusted EBITDA(1) increased by 7.4% to $254.3 million, representing 5.4% of net sales.

data.title: Uber extends rally for a seventh straight session
data.description:
Shares of Uber Technologies (UBER) rose 0.6% to close at $76.82 on Wednesday, adding to a six-session winning streak.

The stock has gained around 7% between February 24 and March 3 compared to a 1.07% fall of the S&P 500 during the same period.

The move comes amid recent developments, including coverage of urban policy shifts after a judge ruled the Trump administration’s move to end New York City’s congestion toll was unlawful, continued attention on autonomous driving after Wayve raised $1.2B from backers including Microsoft, Nvidia, and Uber, and updates on Uber’s push into aerial mobility alongside Joby. The company also announced plans to acquire parking app SpotHero, adding to strategic expansion efforts during the period.

According to Seeking Alpha’s Quant Rating system, UBER is rated Hold, with a score of 3.12 out of 5, receiving an A+ for profitability, but offset by a D- in terms of momentum.

A Seeking Alpha analysis said Uber has become a volatile “battleground” stock as investors debate whether autonomous vehicles represent a threat or tailwind, adding that “the unit economics of AV technology could offer a tailwind for Uber Technologies.”

The analyst noted that “70% of contra-revenue… expenses are driver-related,” arguing that eliminating human-driver costs could lift profitability, and pointed out that Uber’s “200 million+ user network” gives it leverage with AV partners. The note also highlighted diversification, stating that “approximately 50% of total revenue is generated internationally” and “approximately 50% of total gross bookings is generated through the delivery segment.”

On Wall Street, 47 of 56 analysts rate the stock Buy or higher, eight recommend Hold, and one recommends a Strong Sell.

Shares have gained around 1.85% in the past one month, and is down approximately 7.15% year-to-date.

data.title: Netflix shares uptick for seven consecutive sessions; add over 25% in last six sessions
data.description:
Netflix (NFLX) shares clocked seven straight sessions of gains, as the stock rose 1.1% at $98.75 on Wednesday.

The movies and entertainment giant gained 25.2% in the preceding six sessions. The stock has added over 5% in the year.

NFLX is up 19% over the past one month.

Netflix abruptly withdrew from its bid for Warner Bros. Discovery (WBD) in late February, many in Hollywood were caught off guard. 

Most recently, Netflix announced that it is expanding its Ads Suite to give brands more options to buy and measure ads, with a focus on better targeting, frequency management, and scaled reach on its ad-supported plan. 

"Management’s disciplined approach avoids integration risk, preserves balance sheet strength, and positions Netflix for gradual, compelling standalone improvements," pointed out a recent Seeking Alpha analysis.

Looking at Seeking Alpha's Quant Rating, NFLX has a Buy rating with a score of 3.1 out of 5. The company received A+ in the prospect of profitability, while it received D- in valuation.

Turning to the Wall Street community, 36 analysts gave NFLX a Buy and above rating. 11 analysts have given the stock a Hold recommendation, while one recommended Sell.

Seeking Alpha analysts are positive and see the stock as a Buy.

data.title: Rayonier Advanced Materials targets positive free cash flow and substantial EBITDA growth in 2026 through pricing and portfolio initiatives
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Rayonier Advanced Materials (RYAM) Q4 2025

Management View

  • CEO Scott Sutton opened by identifying negative free cash flow of $88 million and high-cost debt as unsustainable, declaring "priority 1...is simple: deliver positive free cash flow in 2026" and stating the company's aim "to exit 2026 with significant momentum with a heavy focus on execution." Sutton emphasized progress in Cellulose Specialties, reporting that "85% of the Specialties business is now arranged at an average price increase of 18% over 2025 with expected volume loss of about 20% compared with 2025," while negotiations on the remaining 15% are ongoing and expected to yield even higher price increases. Sutton also underscored a broad, company-wide playbook to improve EBITDA in 2026 relative to 2025, targeting improvement in every business segment through leadership initiatives, active portfolio management, and new product commercialization.
  • CFO Marcus Moeltner stated, "improved performance based on execution on those themes will position RYAM for refi to really address the capital structure and drive down our interest expense and fixed charges."

Outlook

  • Sutton outlined the plan to deliver positive free cash flow and achieve "a full year EBITDA substantially better than 2025, along with solid positive free cash flow." The company expects to bridge a near zero EBITDA first quarter as new leadership initiatives take effect, with every business expected to improve its EBITDA performance over 2025.
  • Sutton described ongoing negotiations for the remaining 15% of Specialties business, indicating that any successful agreements will come at a price increase significantly higher than the 18% already achieved.
  • The CEO described a "NASCAR leaderboard strategy" for managing product mix dynamically, optimizing asset utilization, and maximizing contribution profit by shifting production across market segments as conditions warrant.

Financial Results

  • Sutton reported that free cash flow for 2025 was negative $88 million and stressed the need to reverse this in the coming year.
  • Management described achievements in pricing, particularly the near 20% price increase for ether-grade cellulose in Europe despite demand challenges, and more than 18% price increases in nitration-grade cellulose.
  • There were no explicit EPS or revenue figures stated for the quarter in the transcript.

Q&A

  • Daniel Harriman, Sidoti, asked about internal confidence in earnings power and long-term value, and whether higher pricing in Cellulose Specialties could attract competitors. Sutton responded that "our best opportunity really comes from the team" and highlighted the company's unique position as the last two domestic producers, noting, "we've got a long way to go before we get to reinvestment economics."
  • Matthew McKellar, RBC, inquired about rejecting a potential offer and the rationale for remaining independent. Sutton declined to comment on specific offers but said, "we have plans that will deliver substantially more value" and outlined four core value creation themes centering on leadership initiatives, dynamic asset allocation, new product development, and inflation offset strategies. Moeltner added that improved execution would support refinancing efforts.
  • McKellar asked about demand and competition dynamics. Sutton described ether-grade cellulose as "challenged a bit" in Europe due to Chinese imports but said RYAM still achieved near 20% price increases. For nitration-grade, there is "lots of new inquiries" and demand, especially for domestic propellant producers.
  • Dmitry Silversteyn, Water Tower Research, asked about strategy for Biomaterials following the withdrawal from the Georgia energy project and operational issues at Tartas. Sutton stressed an integrated approach across all businesses and cited plans to run Tartas harder to increase feedstock for Biomaterials, particularly for the BioNova JV. Silversteyn also queried the timeline for trade remedy cases; Sutton expects a preliminary countervailing duties determination in March and antidumping duties in May.
  • Harriman asked about the longer-term portfolio fit for Paperboard and High-Yield Pulp. Sutton stated, "we're not selling any business, and we're not closing any assets" and expects improvement in both segments through new product commercialization and recovery from oversupply.
  • McKellar followed up on the integrated model strategy and Paperboard opportunities following a competitor's mill closure. Sutton and Moeltner explained the flexible, margin-maximizing approach and noted improved volumes and quality in Paperboard, while also highlighting the need to absorb new U.S. capacity.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts expressed persistent concerns about the sustainability of recent price increases, competitive dynamics, and execution of strategic initiatives. Questions were pointed but constructive, with a focus on unlocking value and mitigating external risks.
  • Management tone was determined and pragmatic in prepared remarks, but at times defensive and measured during Q&A, particularly regarding past performance, pricing ceilings, and portfolio divestitures. Sutton repeatedly pointed to the company's adaptability and indicated, "we have really urgent work to do."
  • Compared to the previous quarter, management's tone shifted from cautious optimism and a focus on overcoming headwinds to urgency and an execution-centric message. Analysts shifted from immediate operational concerns toward probing the credibility and sustainability of the new leadership's plans and pricing achievements.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • The CEO transition from De Lyle Bloomquist to Scott Sutton marked a change in leadership emphasis, with Sutton prioritizing urgent execution, free cash flow, and a holistic value maximization strategy versus Bloomquist's focus on cost reductions, asset divestitures, and high-return project development.
  • Guidance language shifted from a specific EBITDA range and project milestones to an execution-driven plan to "deliver positive free cash flow in 2026" and improve EBITDA across all businesses. The new CEO downplayed asset divestiture, favoring improvement and retention.
  • Strategic focus evolved from targeted cost cuts and asset sales to dynamic portfolio management and new product commercialization across all segments.
  • Analysts' tone remains skeptical but interested, with more focus on the credibility of management’s new approach and the ability to deliver on ambitious pricing and margin targets.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management highlighted the unsustainability of negative free cash flow and high-cost debt, making positive free cash flow in 2026 a mission-critical target.
  • Pricing sustainability in Cellulose Specialties remains a risk, with the CEO noting, "we've got a long way to go before we get to reinvestment economics."
  • Competitive pressure from subsidized imports, especially from China in ethers and from Brazil and Norway in specialty cellulose, is a significant challenge. Trade remedy investigations are ongoing, with preliminary determinations expected in the coming months.
  • Operational challenges at Tartas are being addressed by a crisis management team, with some early success but continued risk.
  • The remaining 15% of Cellulose Specialties business is not yet secured and may result in further volume loss or require higher pricing to close.

Final Takeaway

Rayonier Advanced Materials management is focused on delivering positive free cash flow in 2026, asserting leadership in Cellulose Specialties through substantial price increases and targeted volume management, and driving EBITDA improvements across all business segments through execution of leadership initiatives, dynamic asset allocation, and new product commercialization. The company is addressing high-cost debt and operational challenges while navigating external risks from trade and competition, with the intent to exit 2026 with significant momentum and a streamlined, value-maximizing portfolio.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Most and least shorted energy stocks with over $2B market cap as of Feb
data.description:
In February, the S&P 500 energy sector (XLE) moved nearly 12% in the upward direction this year supported by a 8.3% rise in crude oil futures (CL1:COM) over potential disruption in global crude supply.

Against this backdrop, short interest trends among mid, large and mega-cap energy stocks show a wide divergence. Solaris Energy Infrastructure (SEI) was the most shorted stock in February, while MPLX (MPLX) was the least shorted stock.

Most shorted energy stocks as of February end:

  • Solaris Energy Infrastructure (SEI) 24.05%
  • Centrus Energy (LEU) 23.63%
  • Northern Oil and Gas (NOG) 19.17%
  • Delek US Holdings (DK) 18.12%
  • PBF Energy (PBF) 16.13%
 

Least shorted energy stocks as of February end:

  • MPLX (MPLX) 0.74%
  • Energy Transfer (ET) 0.85%
  • Alliance Resource Partners (ARLP) 0.95%
  • Enterprise Products Partners (EPD) 1.00%
  • Mach Natural Resources (MNR) 1.02%

Energy ETFs: (XLE), (AMLP), (VDE), (XOP), (OIH), and (IXC).
